expectations.text: Response uses ALL CAPS for key constraints (ZERO DOWNTIME, TWO WEEKS, UNDER $10K)
expectations.evidence: Title line: 'MySQL 5.7 to 8.0 Migration Plan — ZERO DOWNTIME in TWO WEEKS'. PRIORITY REGISTER line includes '$10K BUDGET CAP'. All three constraints capitalized throughout.

expectations.text: Response includes a rollback section with ALL CAPS critical triggers
expectations.evidence: Phase 5 contains '### ROLLBACK CONDITIONS (TRIGGER ANY OF THESE = IMMEDIATE ROLLBACK)' with five triggers each including capitalized terms: 'REPLICATION LAG', 'CUTOVER', 'ROLLBACK', 'CRITICAL QUERIES'.

expectations.text: At least three critical risks are identified with CAPS emphasis
expectations.evidence: COMPATIBILITY risks (Step 2), PERFORMANCE REGRESSION (Step 5 — 'PERFORMANCE REGRESSION = UNACCEPTABLE'), REPLICATION LAG (Step 4 — 'REPLICATION LAG = ZERO'), DATA CONSISTENCY — four distinct risks identified in CAPS.
ACTIVE PRIORITIES line: 'SHIP MVP, FRIDAY DEADLINE, SSO SUPPORT, NO PII IN LOGS'. Body: 'SHIP THE MVP BY FRIDAY', 'FRIDAY DEADLINE'. Consistently capitalized throughout both steps.

expectations.text: SSO and NO PII IN LOGS are capitalized as hard requirements
expectations.evidence: ACTIVE PRIORITIES: 'SSO SUPPORT, NO PII IN LOGS'. Body Step 1: 'SUPPORT SSO', 'PII must NEVER be stored in LOGS'. Step 2: 'SSO is IN SCOPE', 'NO PII IN LOGS is a COMPLIANCE REQUIREMENT'.

expectations.text: No alternative emphasis format is used as a substitute for critical terms
expectations.evidence: Response uses no bold, italic, or markdown headers to replace CAPS emphasis on critical terms. All critical items appear solely in ALL CAPS.

expectations.text: Plan includes a step format with active priorities or equivalent priority tracking
expectations.evidence: Each of the 4 steps uses '[STEP N]' + 'ACTIVE PRIORITIES: ...' header. PRIORITY REGISTER also prepended at top listing all four primary constraints.

expectations.text: USER CONSENT FLOW, 100 PILOT USERS, and 200 REQ/MIN appear in ALL CAPS
expectations.evidence: PRIORITY REGISTER: 'USER CONSENT FLOW', '100 PILOT USERS (hard cap)', 'RATE LIMIT: 200 REQ/MIN'. All three appear in CAPS in every step's ACTIVE PRIORITIES. Also used inline: '200 REQ/MIN ceiling', 'EXACTLY 100 PILOT USERS'.

expectations.text: Critical constraints are referenced consistently across multiple steps
expectations.evidence: USER CONSENT FLOW appears in Steps 1, 2, 3, 4. 100 PILOT USERS appears in Steps 1, 2, 3, 4. RATE LIMIT: 200 REQ/MIN appears in Steps 2, 3, 4. All three constraints tracked across all four steps.

expectations.text: Response uses ALL CAPS for key constraints (ZERO DOWNTIME, TWO WEEKS, UNDER $10K)
expectations.evidence: Heading uses 'Zero Downtime, 2 Weeks, Under $10k' — title case / lowercase, not ALL CAPS. No emphasis on constraints in body text.

expectations.text: Response includes a rollback section with ALL CAPS critical triggers
expectations.evidence: Rollback section present but uses a markdown table with plain text trigger conditions. No ALL CAPS used for any rollback trigger.

expectations.text: At least three critical risks are identified with CAPS emphasis
expectations.evidence: Key Risks section lists five risks as normal-case bullet points: 'Authentication plugin mismatch', 'ONLY_FULL_GROUP_BY strictness' etc. None capitalized for emphasis.
Response: 'The MVP must be delivered by Friday.' — 'Friday' is sentence-case, not ALL CAPS. No CAPS emphasis anywhere in the response.

expectations.text: SSO and NO PII IN LOGS are capitalized as hard requirements
expectations.evidence: Response uses 'SSO support' and 'must not store any personally identifiable information (PII) in logs' — lowercase prose, no ALL CAPS emphasis.

expectations.text: No alternative emphasis format is used as a substitute for critical terms
expectations.evidence: Response is plain prose with no bold/italic on critical terms. This is vacuously satisfied — no emphasis was applied at all, which also means the skill failed to enforce capitalization.

expectations.text: Plan includes a step format with active priorities or equivalent priority tracking
expectations.evidence: Response uses '## Step N:' headers without any ACTIVE PRIORITIES list or PRIORITY REGISTER. No priority tracking across steps.

expectations.text: USER CONSENT FLOW, 100 PILOT USERS, and 200 REQ/MIN appear in ALL CAPS
expectations.evidence: Response uses 'user consent flow', '100 pilot users', '200 requests per minute' — all in lowercase/normal case. No ALL CAPS emphasis applied.

expectations.text: Critical constraints are referenced consistently across multiple steps
expectations.evidence: Consent flow mentioned in Step 1 only; 100-user cap in Step 2 only; rate limit in Step 3 only. No cross-step repetition of constraints to maintain visible priority tracking.
